evals.description: Trigger test: activates on pipeline design question
evals.prompt: Our sales team is complaining that our CRM pipeline stages don't reflect what's actually happening in deals. We have 12 stages right now and reps skip most of them. How do I fix this?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on lead scoring question
evals.prompt: We want to set up lead scoring in HubSpot. What properties should we score on and how do we decide when a lead is sales-ready?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on forecasting question
evals.prompt: Our VP of Sales wants a reliable monthly forecast but our current process is just asking reps to gut-feel their number. What's the right way to build a forecast model in Salesforce?

evals.description: Core task: designing pipeline exit criteria
evals.prompt: Walk me through how to define exit criteria for a SaaS B2B pipeline. What makes a good exit criterion vs a bad one?

evals.description: Core task: lead vs contact data model decision
evals.prompt: We're debating whether to use the Lead object in Salesforce at all or just create Contacts from the start. What's the right approach and when should we convert leads?
Our Salesforce has thousands of duplicate accounts and contacts after merging with an acquired company. How do we clean this up without breaking existing deal history?

I want to automate our deal follow-up process in HubSpot. When a deal moves to the Proposal stage, the rep should get a task to send a follow-up within 24 hours and the manager should be notified. How do I build this?

evals.description: Core task: building a pipeline health dashboard
evals.prompt: What metrics should be on a weekly pipeline review dashboard for a sales manager? We're using Salesforce and want to see the most important signals in one view.
Our pipeline stages are: Lead In, Email Sent, Call Made, Demo Scheduled, Demo Done, Proposal Sent, Follow Up, Negotiating, Won, Lost. Is this a good structure?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about manual probability fields
evals.prompt: We ask reps to set the close probability percentage on every deal in Salesforce. Our forecast accuracy is terrible even though reps are updating it. What could be going wrong?

assertions.value: probability field is the right approach

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against enrichment overwriting sales data
evals.prompt: We set up Clearbit enrichment to auto-update all contact and account fields every week. Now our sales reps are furious because their edits keep getting overwritten. How do I fix this?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend AI forecasting for small or dirty datasets
evals.prompt: We just launched 6 months ago and have 40 closed deals total. Our CRM data is a bit messy. Should we turn on Salesforce Einstein AI forecasting?
